⭐ Health and Physical Education

Athletics and Gymnastics

Run, jump, flip, and tumble - the exciting world of athletics and gymnastics!

📚 Unit 7 ⏱️ 10 min read

Unit 7: Athletics and Gymnastics

Athletics and gymnastics are sports that test strength, speed, flexibility, and coordination.

Lesson 1: Athletics

What is Athletics?

Athletics (also called track and field) includes running, jumping, and throwing events.

Categories of Athletics

CategoryEventsSkills needed
Track eventsRunning racesSpeed, endurance
Field eventsJumping and throwingPower, technique

Track Events (Running)

Types of Running Races

RaceDistanceWhat it tests
Sprint100m, 200mSpeed, explosive power
Middle distance400m, 800mSpeed + endurance
Long distance1500m, 3000mEndurance, stamina
Relay race4×100m, 4×400mTeamwork + speed
Hurdles100m (with hurdles)Speed + jumping

Starting Positions in Running

CommandPosition
”On your marks”Crouch down, hands behind the start line
”Set”Lift hips, shift weight forward
”Go” (or gunshot)Push off and start running

Proper Running Technique

Body partCorrect form
HeadStraight, looking forward
ShouldersRelaxed
ArmsSwing forward and backward (not across body)
HandsRelaxed, not fists
LegsKnees lift, feet land on midfoot/toe
BreathingDeep and rhythmic

💡 Tip: Do not look back at other runners – run your own race!

Relay Race (Team Event)

RuleExplanation
Team4 runners per team
BatonMust be passed within the exchange zone
DisqualificationDropping the baton or passing outside the zone
OrderEach runner runs one leg of the race

Field Events

Jumping Events

EventHow it worksKey skill
Long jumpRun and jump into a sand pitSpeed + explosive jump
High jumpJump over a horizontal barFlexibility + technique
Triple jumpHop, step, then jump into sand pitCoordination + power
Long Jump Technique
PhaseAction
Approach runBuild speed on the runway
TakeoffJump off one foot from the takeoff board
FlightBody position in the air (hitch kick or hang)
LandingLand in the sand pit with feet forward
High Jump Technique (Scissors/Fosbury Flop)
PhaseAction
ApproachRun in a curve
TakeoffJump off the outside foot
ClearanceGo over the bar backward (Flop)
LandingLand on the mat

Throwing Events

EventEquipmentTechnique
Shot putHeavy metal ball (shot)Push from neck, not throw
JavelinSpear-like stickThrow over the shoulder
DiscusHeavy round plateSpin and release
Cricket ball throwCricket ballOverarm throw for distance
Shot Put Technique
StepAction
1Hold the shot at the neck (not palm)
2Face away from the throwing direction (glide position)
3Glide or spin to the front
4Push the shot upward and outward
5Release at a 40-degree angle
6Stay inside the circle after throw

💡 Safety: Never throw in the direction of other people. Always check that the area is clear.

Athletics Rules and Etiquette

RuleWhy
Stay in your lane (for track events)Fairness, avoid collisions
Do not false startFair start for everyone
Respect officialsThey enforce rules
Do not interfere with other athletesDisqualification
Stay behind the line in field eventsSafety

Lesson 2: Gymnastics and Their Supporting Activities

What is Gymnastics?

Gymnastics is a sport that involves exercises requiring strength, flexibility, balance, and coordination.

Types of Gymnastics

TypeDescriptionEquipment
Artistic gymnasticsRoutines on apparatusFloor, vault, bars, beam
Rhythmic gymnasticsDance with equipmentRibbon, hoop, ball, rope
Acrobatic gymnasticsPartner/group balancesMat
TrampolineBouncing and flipsTrampoline

Basic Gymnastics Positions

PositionDescriptionImage (described)
TuckKnees pulled to chest, hands holding shinsLike a ball
PikeLegs straight, body folded at hipsLike a “V” shape
StraddleLegs straight and apart, body foldedLike a wide “V”
LayoutBody completely straightLike a pencil

Basic Gymnastics Movements (Floor)

1. Forward Roll (Somersault)

StepAction
1Squat down, hands on the floor
2Tuck chin to chest
3Push with legs and roll forward on the back
4Stand up without using hands

💡 Tip: Roll on the spine, not the head!

2. Backward Roll

StepAction
1Squat down, hands beside ears (fingers pointing back)
2Push with hands and roll backward
3Place hands flat on the floor
4Push up to standing

3. Cartwheel

StepAction
1Stand with arms raised
2Place one hand on the floor
3Kick legs over the body
4Place second hand on the floor
5Land one foot, then the other
6End standing sideways

4. Handstand (against wall for beginners)

StepAction
1Stand facing away from wall
2Place hands on floor, one leg up
3Kick the other leg up toward the wall
4Keep body straight
5Lower feet slowly

5. Bridge

StepAction
1Lie on back, knees bent
2Place hands beside ears (fingers pointing toward shoulders)
3Push up with arms and legs
4Arch the back, head down
5Look at the floor between hands

Vault

StepAction
1Run toward the vaulting table
2Jump onto the springboard
3Place hands on the vault
4Push off and straddle/pike legs
5Land on the mat

Balance Beam (Basic skills)

SkillAction
WalkHeel-toe walking
TurnPivot on one foot
JumpSmall jumps, land softly
StretchLeg lifts and holds

Gymnastics Safety

RuleWhy
Always use a matCushions falls
Have a spotterAn adult or friend to catch you
Warm up firstPrevents muscle pulls
Do not try advanced moves aloneRisk of serious injury
Wear proper clothesLeotard or tight clothes (nothing loose)

Supporting Activities for Gymnastics

These activities help build the strength and flexibility needed for gymnastics:

ActivityHow it helps
Skipping ropeFootwork, coordination
StretchingFlexibility
Push-upsUpper body strength
Sit-upsCore strength
Balance on one legBalance
Climbing ropes or laddersGrip and arm strength
Holding a plankCore stability

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Athletics has two categories: ______ events and ______ events.
  2. A ______ race has 4 runners passing a baton.
  3. In long jump, you land in a ______ pit.
  4. In shot put, the ball is held at the ______.
  5. A forward roll is also called a ______.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. Sprint races test endurance more than speed. (T/F)
  2. In high jump, you jump over a bar. (T/F)
  3. A cartwheel is a gymnastics movement. (T/F)
  4. You should always have a spotter for gymnastics. (T/F)
  5. The javelin is a running event. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Event to the Category

EventCategory
100m sprinta) Field event (jumping)
Long jumpb) Track event
Shot putc) Field event (throwing)
Relay raced) Track event

Exercise 4: Name the Gymnastics Position

Describe the position.

  1. Body completely straight like a pencil → ______
  2. Knees pulled to chest, like a ball → ______
  3. Legs straight, body folded at hips → ______

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. track, field; 2. relay, 3. sand, 4. neck, 5. somersault

Exercise 2: 1. F, 2. T, 3. T, 4. T, 5. F

Exercise 3: 100m sprint→b, Long jump→a, Shot put→c, Relay race→d

Exercise 4: 1. Layout, 2. Tuck, 3. Pike

Great work! Run fast, jump high, and flip with joy. Athletics and gymnastics are fun and healthy!
